content.problemStatement:
Write a program to check if the given number NNN is Armstrong number.

**Note:** Armstrong number is a number that is equal to the sum of its own digits each raised to the power of the number of digits.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains a single integer, NNN - The number that needs to be checked.


Output Format

Print on a single line:

`YES` if NNN is Armstrong number.
`NO` otherwise.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print YES if N is an Armstrong number, otherwise NO. Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 153 has 3 digits. 1^3 + 5^3 + 3^3 = 153, so it is an Armstrong number.

solution.approach: An Armstrong (narcissistic) number equals the sum of its digits each raised to the power of the number of digits. For each test case we count the digits d of N, then compute sum = Σ (digit^d) using integer arithmetic. If sum equals N the answer is YES, otherwise NO. Counting digits and the summation are both O(number of digits) ≤ 7, so the whole solution runs in O(T) time with O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * D) where D ≤ 7 (number of digits of N), effectively O(T).
